claim.content: For the paramagnetic (unpolarized) 2D HEG over the density-parameter range \\(r_{s}=1\\) to 10, let \\(m^{*}(\\infty)\\) denote the quasiparticle effective mass extrapolated to the thermodynamic limit by fitting the finite-size DMC effective masses to the single-power-law form \\(m^{*}(N)=m^{*}(\\infty)+b\\,N^{-\\gamma}\\) and adopting the exponent \\(\\gamma=3/2\\) for the extrapolation. The proposition is: the extrapolated numerical values \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=1)=0.955(2)\\), \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=5)=1.04(2)\\), and \\(m^{*}(r_{s}=10)=1.03(4)\\) indicate that the true thermodynamic-limit quasiparticle effective mass of the clean continuum-model paramagnetic 2D HEG remains close to unity across this \\(r_{s}\\) range and that there is no significant enhancement of \\(m^{*}\\) at low density for this physical system class.
